# Organiser Architecture for Resilient Communities

## 10.1 Events Team

### Core Function
Responsible for end-to-end event execution from planning through post-event follow-up.

### Team Structure
- **Event Coordinator** (Lead role): Overall event planning and logistics
- **Venue Manager**: Relationships with hosting organizations and space management
- **Registration Coordinator**: Attendee management and communication
- **Day-of-Event Team**: Setup, logistics, and on-site coordination

### Key Responsibilities
- Monthly event planning and scheduling
- Venue booking and logistics coordination  
- Registration management and attendee communication
- Equipment and catering arrangements
- Event day execution and volunteer coordination
- Post-event cleanup and feedback collection

### Skills and Qualifications
- Project management and organizational skills
- Vendor and venue relationship management
- Crisis management and problem-solving
- Communication and coordination abilities
- Technical understanding for AV and equipment needs

## 10.2 Speaker Pipeline Team

### Core Function
Building and maintaining sustainable speaker ecosystems that reduce organizer workload while improving content quality.

### Team Structure
- **Speaker Coordinator** (Lead role): Strategic speaker pipeline management
- **Content Curator**: Talk quality and community fit assessment
- **Speaker Mentors** (2-3 people): First-time speaker development and coaching
- **Corporate Liaison**: Employee speaker relationships with partner companies

### Key Responsibilities
- Proactive speaker sourcing and relationship building
- Speaker onboarding and content development support
- Presentation coaching and feedback systems
- Speaker database maintenance and performance tracking
- Corporate speaker program development
- Speaking skills workshops and training

### Skills and Qualifications
- Industry knowledge and professional networks
- Presentation skills coaching and development
- Content evaluation and curation abilities
- Relationship building and communication
- Technical expertise to assess presentation quality

## 10.3 Industry Partnerships Team

### Core Function  
Developing mutually beneficial relationships with corporate organizations for venues, speakers, sponsorship, and community growth.

### Team Structure
- **Partnership Manager** (Lead role): Strategic relationship development
- **Corporate Outreach Coordinator**: New partnership identification and development
- **Venue Partnership Specialist**: Dedicated venue relationship management
- **Sponsor Relations Coordinator**: Financial and in-kind sponsorship coordination

### Key Responsibilities
- Corporate partnership strategy and development
- Venue partnership negotiation and management
- Sponsorship relationship development and stewardship
- Corporate employee engagement programs
- Industry trend monitoring and community positioning
- Partnership performance measurement and optimization

### Skills and Qualifications
- Business development and relationship management experience
- Corporate communication and negotiation skills
- Understanding of sponsorship and partnership models
- Industry knowledge and professional credibility
- Strategic thinking and long-term relationship building

## 10.4 University Outreach Team

### Core Function
Bridging academic and industry communities to support student development and faculty engagement.

### Team Structure
- **Academic Liaison** (Lead role): University relationship development
- **Student Engagement Coordinator**: Student-focused programming and support
- **Faculty Relations Specialist**: Academic speaker and collaboration development
- **Career Development Advisor**: Student career guidance and industry connections

### Key Responsibilities
- University partnership development and maintenance
- Student participation and engagement programs
- Faculty speaker recruitment and research collaboration
- Career development and job placement support
- Academic-industry project facilitation
- Curriculum influence and educational program development

### Skills and Qualifications
- Academic background and university experience
- Career development and mentorship experience
- Understanding of academic-industry collaboration
- Student engagement and program development
- Research and educational background

## 10.5 Marketing and Community Team

### Core Function
Building community brand, managing external communications, and driving community growth through strategic marketing.

### Team Structure
- **Marketing Manager** (Lead role): Brand strategy and campaign development
- **Social Media Coordinator**: Day-to-day social media management and engagement
- **Content Creator**: Blog posts, graphics, and educational content development
- **Community Engagement Specialist**: Member communication and relationship building

### Key Responsibilities
- Brand development and message consistency
- Social media strategy and content calendar
- Event promotion and marketing campaign execution
- Community newsletter and communication management
- Website content and search engine optimization
- Media relations and external communication

### Skills and Qualifications
- Marketing and communication experience
- Social media management and content creation
- Brand development and design capabilities
- Community management and engagement
- Technical writing and content development

## 10.6 Infrastructure Team

### Core Function
Maintaining technical systems, digital assets, and community knowledge repository.

### Team Structure
- **Infrastructure Maintainer** (Lead role): Technical systems and architecture
- **Website Administrator**: Community website and online presence
- **Documentation Manager**: Community knowledge and process documentation
- **Data Manager**: Community analytics and database management

### Key Responsibilities
- Community website and platform maintenance
- GitHub organization and code repository management
- Event documentation and slide archive management
- Community data analysis and reporting
- Technical tool evaluation and implementation
- Security and privacy compliance management

### Skills and Qualifications
- Technical systems administration experience
- Web development and platform management
- Documentation and knowledge management
- Data analysis and reporting capabilities
- Security and privacy best practices understanding

## Team Coordination and Integration

### Cross-Team Communication
- **Monthly All-Hands Meetings**: Strategic alignment and update sharing
- **Project-Based Collaboration**: Joint initiatives requiring multiple teams  
- **Shared Documentation**: Central knowledge base and communication channels
- **Inter-Team Mentoring**: Skills sharing and cross-functional development

### Resource Sharing
- **Budget Coordination**: Shared financial resources and expense management
- **Volunteer Pool**: Flexible volunteer assignment across teams as needed
- **Expertise Exchange**: Leveraging skills across teams for community benefit
- **Tool and Platform Sharing**: Common infrastructure and systems access

### Quality Assurance
- **Performance Metrics**: Team-specific and community-wide success measurement
- **Regular Review Sessions**: Team effectiveness and improvement planning
- **Community Feedback Integration**: Attendee and member input incorporation
- **Best Practice Documentation**: Continuous improvement and knowledge capture

## Scaling and Adaptation

### Team Size Guidelines
- **Stage 2 Community** (30-60 attendees): 1-2 people per team, some combined roles
- **Stage 3 Community** (60-120 attendees): 2-4 people per team, specialized roles
- **Stage 4 Community** (120+ attendees): Full teams with sub-specialization

### Role Evolution
Teams adapt responsibilities based on community growth phase, external opportunities, and member interests. Flexibility in team structure enables response to changing community needs and organizer availability.

### Success Metrics
- **Team Performance**: Delivery quality and timeline adherence
- **Community Impact**: Growth metrics and member satisfaction
- **Organizer Development**: Skills growth and leadership advancement
- **Sustainability**: Volunteer retention and succession planning effectiveness

This organizer architecture provides structure while maintaining flexibility, ensuring that PyData Johannesburg can scale effectively while preserving the community values and quality that drive long-term success.
